heading: Eligibility for assisted housing

children.heading: Income eligibility for public housing
children.label: a Income eligibility for public housing

children.heading: Income mix within projects
children.label: 1 Income mix within projects
children.text: A public housing agency may establish and utilize income-mix criteria for the selection of residents for dwelling units in public housing projects, subject to the requirements of this section.

children.heading: .—1So in original. No subpar. (B) has been enacted.1Targeting
children.label: A .—1So in original. No subpar. (B) has been enacted.1Targeting
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (4), of the public housing dwelling units of a public housing agency made available for occupancy in any fiscal year by eligible families, not less than 40 percent shall be occupied by extremely low-income families.

children.heading: Prohibition of concentration of low-income families
children.label: 3 Prohibition of concentration of low-income families

children.heading: Prohibition
children.label: A Prohibition
children.text: A public housing agency may not, in complying with the requirements under paragraph (2), concentrate very low-income families (or other families with relatively low incomes) in public housing dwelling units in certain public housing projects or certain buildings within projects. The Secretary shall review the income and occupancy characteristics of the public housing projects and the buildings of such projects of such agencies to ensure compliance with the provisions of this paragraph and paragraph (2).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: A public housing agency shall submit with its annual public housing agency plan under an admissions policy designed to provide for deconcentration of poverty and income-mixing by bringing higher income tenants into lower income projects and lower income tenants into higher income projects. This clause may not be construed to impose or require any specific income or racial quotas for any project or projects.section 1437c–1 of this title

children.heading: Incentives
children.label: ii Incentives
children.text: In implementing the policy under clause (i), a public housing agency may offer incentives for eligible families having higher incomes to occupy dwelling unit in projects predominantly occupied by eligible families having lower incomes, and provide for occupancy of eligible families having lower incomes in projects predominantly occupied by eligible families having higher incomes.

children.heading: Family choice
children.label: iii Family choice
children.text: Incentives referred to in clause (ii) may be made available by a public housing agency only in a manner that allows for the eligible family to have the sole discretion in determining whether to accept the incentive and an agency may not take any adverse action toward any eligible family for choosing not to accept an incentive and occupancy of a project described in clause (i)(II), , That the skipping of a family on a waiting list to reach another family to implement the policy under clause (i) shall not be considered an adverse action. An agency implementing an admissions policy under this subparagraph shall implement the policy in a manner that does not prevent or interfere with the use of site-based waiting lists authorized under section 1437d(s) of this title.23So in original. Cl. (i) does not contain subclauses.2See References in Text note below.3Provided

children.heading: Authority
children.label: A Authority
children.text: Except as provided under subparagraph (D), the number of public housing dwelling units that a public housing agency shall otherwise make available in accordance with paragraph (2)(A) to comply with the percentage requirement under such paragraph for a fiscal year shall be reduced by the credit number for the agency under subparagraph (B).

children.heading: Credit for exceeding tenant-based assistance targeting requirement
children.label: B Credit for exceeding tenant-based assistance targeting requirement
i
children.text: the aggregate number of qualified families who, in such fiscal year, are initially provided tenant-based assistance under by the agency; exceedssection 1437f of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the number of qualified families that is required for the agency to comply with the percentage requirement under subsection (b)(1) for such fiscal year.

children.label: i
children.text: the number of dwelling units that is equivalent to 10 percent of the aggregate number of families initially provided tenant-based assistance under by the agency in such fiscal year; orsection 1437f of this title

children.label: I
children.text: are in projects that are located in census tracts having a poverty rate of 30 percent or more; and

children.label: II
children.text: are made available for occupancy during such fiscal year and are actually filled only by families whose incomes at the time of commencement of such occupancy exceed 30 percent of the area median income, as determined by the Secretary with adjustments for smaller and larger families.

children.heading: Fungibility floor
children.label: D Fungibility floor
children.text: Notwithstanding any authority under subparagraph (A), of the public housing dwelling units of a public housing agency made available for occupancy in any fiscal year by eligible families, not less than 30 percent shall be occupied by families whose incomes at the time of commencement of occupancy do not exceed 30 percent of the area median income, as determined by the Secretary with adjustments for smaller and larger families.

children.heading: Qualified family
children.label: E Qualified family
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “qualified family” means a family having an income described in subsection (b)(1).

children.heading: Limitations on tenancy for over-income families
children.label: 5 Limitations on tenancy for over-income families
I
children.text: the applicable fair market rental established under for a dwelling unit in the same market area of the same size; orsection 1437f(c) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: the amount of the monthly subsidy provided under this chapter for the dwelling unit, which shall include any amounts from the Operating Fund and Capital Fund under used for the unit, as determined by the agency in accordance with regulations that the Secretary shall issue to carry out this subclause; orsection 1437g of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: terminate the tenancy of such family in public housing not later than 6 months after the income determination described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Notice
children.label: B Notice
children.text: In the case of any family residing in a dwelling unit of public housing whose income for a year has exceeded the applicable income limitation under subparagraph (C), upon the conclusion of such year the public housing agency shall provide written notice to such family of the requirements under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Income limitation
children.label: C Income limitation
children.text: The income limitation under this subparagraph shall be 120 percent of the median income for the area, as determined by the Secretary with adjustments for smaller and larger families, except that the Secretary may establish income limitations higher or lower than 120 percent of such median income on the basis of the Secretary’s findings that such variations are necessary because of prevailing levels of construction costs, or unusually high or low family incomes, vacancy rates, or rental costs.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: D Exception
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to a family occupying a dwelling unit in public housing pursuant to paragraph (5) of .section 1437a(a) of this title

children.heading: Reports on over-income families and waiting lists
children.label: E Reports on over-income families and waiting lists
I
children.text: the number of families residing, as of the end of the year for which the report is submitted, in public housing administered by the agency who had incomes exceeding the applicable income limitation under subparagraph (C); and

children.label: II
children.text: the number of families, as of the end of such year, on the waiting lists for admission to public housing projects of the agency; and

children.label: ii
children.text: make the information reported pursuant to clause (i) publicly available.

children.heading: Income eligibility for tenant-based section 1437f assistance
children.label: b Income eligibility for tenant-based section 1437f assistance

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Of the families initially provided tenant-based assistance under by a public housing agency in any fiscal year, not less than 75 percent shall be extremely low-income families.section 1437f of this title

children.heading: Jurisdictions served by multiple PHAs
children.label: 2 Jurisdictions served by multiple PHAs
children.text: In the case of any 2 or more public housing agencies that administer tenant-based assistance under with respect solely to identical geographical areas, such agencies shall be treated as a single public housing agency for purposes of paragraph (1).section 1437f of this title

children.heading: Income eligibility for project-based section 1437f assistance
children.label: c Income eligibility for project-based section 1437f assistance

children.heading: Pre-1981 act projects
children.label: 1 Pre-1981 act projects
children.text: Not more than 25 percent of the dwelling units that were available for occupancy under section 8 [] housing assistance payments contracts under this chapter before , and which will be leased on or after , shall be available for leasing by low-income families other than very low-income families.42 U.S.C. 1437fOctober 1, 1981October 1, 1981

children.heading: Post-1981 act projects
children.label: 2 Post-1981 act projects
children.text: Not more than 15 percent of the dwelling units which become available for occupancy under section 8 [] housing assistance payments contracts under this chapter on or after , shall be available for leasing by low-income families other than very low-income families.42 U.S.C. 1437fOctober 1, 1981

children.heading: Targeting
children.label: 3 Targeting
children.text: For each project assisted under a contract for project-based assistance, of the dwelling units that become available for occupancy in any fiscal year that are assisted under the contract, not less than 40 percent shall be available for leasing only by extremely low-income families.

children.heading: Prohibition of skipping
children.label: 4 Prohibition of skipping
children.text: In developing admission procedures implementing paragraphs (1), (2), and (3), the Secretary shall prohibit project owners from selecting families for residence in an order different from the order on the waiting list for the purpose of selecting relatively higher income families for residence. Nothing in this paragraph or this subsection may be construed to prevent an owner of housing assisted under a contract for project-based assistance from establishing a preference for occupancy in such housing for families containing a member who is employed.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: 5 Exception
children.text: The limitations established in paragraphs (1), (2), and (3) shall not apply to dwelling units made available under project-based contracts under for the purpose of preventing displacement, or ameliorating the effects of displacement.section 1437f of this title

children.label: A
children.text: The new construction or substantial rehabilitation program under (as in effect before ).section 1437f(b)(2) of this titleOctober 1, 1983

children.label: B
children.text: The property disposition program under (as in effect before the effective date under section 503(a) of the Quality Housing and Work Responsibility Act of 1998).section 1437f(b) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: The loan management set-aside program under subsections (b) and (v) of .section 1437f of this title

children.label: D
children.text: The project-based certificate program under .section 1437f(d)(2) of this title

children.label: E
children.text: The moderate rehabilitation program under (as in effect before ).section 1437f(e)(2) of this titleOctober 1, 1991

children.label: F
children.text: The low-income housing preservation program under Low-Income Housing Preservation and Resident Homeownership Act of 1990 [ et seq.] or the provisions of the Emergency Low Income Housing Preservation Act of 1987 (as in effect before ).12 U.S.C. 4101November 28, 1990

children.label: G
children.text: (as in effect before the effective date under section 503(a) of the Quality Housing and Work Responsibility Act of 1998), following conversion from assistance under or .Section 1437f of this titlesection 1701s of title 12section 1715z–1(f)(2) of title 12

children.heading: Establishment of different standards
children.label: d Establishment of different standards
children.text: Notwithstanding subsection (a)(2) or (b)(1), if approved by the Secretary, a public housing agency may for good cause establish and implement, in accordance with the public housing agency plan, an admission standard other than the standard under such subsection.

children.heading: Eligibility for assistance based on assets
children.label: e Eligibility for assistance based on assets
A
children.text: whose net family assets exceed $100,000, as such amount is adjusted annually by applying an inflationary factor as the Secretary considers appropriate; or

children.label: i
children.text: any property for which the family is receiving assistance under subsection (y) or ()(12) of ;osection 1437f of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: any person that is a victim of domestic violence; or

children.label: iii
children.text: any family that is offering such property for sale.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “net family assets” means, for all members of the household, the net cash value of all assets after deducting reasonable costs that would be incurred in disposing of real property, savings, stocks, bonds, and other forms of capital investment. Such term does not include interests in Indian trust land, equity in property for which the family is receiving assistance under subsection (y) or ()(12) of , equity accounts in homeownership programs of the Department of Housing and Urban Development, or Family Self Sufficiency accounts.osection 1437f of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the value of personal property, except for items of personal property of significant value, as the Secretary may establish or the public housing agency may determine;

children.label: ii
children.text: the value of any retirement account;

children.label: iii
children.text: real property for which the family does not have the effective legal authority necessary to sell such property;

children.label: iv
children.text: any amounts recovered in any civil action or settlement based on a claim of malpractice, negligence, or other breach of duty owed to a member of the family and arising out of law, that resulted in a member of the family being disabled;

children.label: v
children.text: the value of any Coverdell education savings account under or any qualified tuition program under section 529 of such title; andsection 530 of title 26

children.label: vi
children.text: such other exclusions as the Secretary may establish.

children.heading: Trust funds
children.label: C Trust funds
children.text: In cases in which a trust fund has been established and the trust is not revocable by, or under the control of, any member of the family or household, the value of the trust fund shall not be considered an asset of a family if the fund continues to be held in trust. Any income distributed from the trust fund shall be considered income for purposes of and any calculations of annual family income, except in the case of medical expenses for a minor.section 1437a(b) of this title

children.heading: Net family assets
children.label: A Net family assets
children.text: A public housing agency or owner may determine the net assets of a family, for purposes of this section, based on a certification by the family that the net assets of such family do not exceed $50,000, as such amount is adjusted annually by applying an inflationary factor as the Secretary considers appropriate.

children.heading: No current real property ownership
children.label: B No current real property ownership
children.text: A public housing agency or owner may determine compliance with paragraph (1)(B) based on a certification by the family that such family does not have any current ownership interest in any real property at the time the agency or owner reviews the family’s income.

children.heading: Standardized forms
children.label: C Standardized forms
children.text: The Secretary may develop standardized forms for the certifications referred to in subparagraphs (A) and (B).

children.heading: Compliance for public housing dwelling units
children.label: 4 Compliance for public housing dwelling units
children.text: When recertifying family income with respect to families residing in public housing dwelling units, a public housing agency may, in the discretion of the agency and only pursuant to a policy that is set forth in the public housing agency plan under for the agency, choose not to enforce the limitation under paragraph (1).section 1437c–1 of this title

children.heading: Enforcement
children.label: 5 Enforcement
children.text: When recertifying the income of a family residing in a dwelling unit assisted under this chapter, a public housing agency or owner may choose not to enforce the limitation under paragraph (1) or may establish exceptions to such limitation based on eligibility criteria, but only pursuant to a policy that is set forth in the public housing agency plan under for the agency or under a policy adopted by the owner. Eligibility criteria for establishing exceptions may provide for separate treatment based on family type and may be based on different factors, such as age, disability, income, the ability of the family to find suitable alternative housing, and whether supportive services are being provided.section 1437c–1 of this title

children.heading: Authority to delay evictions
children.label: 6 Authority to delay evictions
children.text: In the case of a family residing in a dwelling unit assisted under this chapter who does not comply with the limitation under paragraph (1), the public housing agency or project owner may delay eviction or termination of the family based on such noncompliance for a period of not more than 6 months.

children.label: A
children.text: Beginning in fiscal year 2018, the Secretary shall require public housing agencies to require each applicant for, or recipient of, benefits under this chapter to provide authorization by the applicant or recipient (or by any other person whose income or resources are material to the determination of the eligibility of the applicant or recipient for such benefits) for the public housing agency to obtain (subject to the cost reimbursement requirements of section 1115(a) of the Right to Financial Privacy Act []) from any financial institution (within the meaning of section 1101(1) of such Act []) any financial record (within the meaning of section 1101(2) of such Act []) held by the institution with respect to the applicant or recipient (or any such other person) whenever the public housing agency determines the record is needed in connection with a determination with respect to such eligibility or the amount of such benefits.12 U.S.C. 341512 U.S.C. 3401(1)12 U.S.C. 3401(2)

children.label: i
children.text: the rendering of a final adverse decision on the applicant’s application for eligibility for benefits under this chapter;

children.label: ii
children.text: the cessation of the recipient’s eligibility for benefits under this chapter; or

children.label: iii
children.text: the express revocation by the applicant or recipient (or such other person referred to in subparagraph (A)) of the authorization, in a written notification to the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: An authorization obtained by the public housing agency pursuant to this paragraph shall be considered to meet the requirements of the Right to Financial Privacy Act [ et seq.] for purposes of section 1103(a) of such Act [], and need not be furnished to the financial institution, notwithstanding section 1104(a) of such Act [].12 U.S.C. 340112 U.S.C. 3403(a)12 U.S.C. 3404(a)

children.label: ii
children.text: The certification requirements of section 1103(b) of the Right to Financial Privacy Act [] shall not apply to requests by the public housing agency pursuant to an authorization provided under this clause.12 U.S.C. 3403(b)

children.label: iii
children.text: A request by the public housing agency pursuant to an authorization provided under this clause is deemed to meet the requirements of section 1104(a)(3) of the Right to Financial Privacy Act [] and the flush language of section 1102 of such Act [].12 U.S.C. 3404(a)(3)12 U.S.C. 3402

children.label: iv
children.text: The public housing agency shall inform any person who provides authorization pursuant to this paragraph of the duration and scope of the authorization.

children.label: D
children.text: If an applicant for, or recipient of, benefits under this chapter (or any such other person referred to in subparagraph (A)) refuses to provide, or revokes, any authorization made by the applicant or recipient for the public housing agency to obtain from any financial institution any financial record, the public housing agency may, on that basis, determine that the applicant or recipient is ineligible for benefits under this subchapter.

children.heading: Ineligibility of individuals convicted of manufacturing or producing methamphetamine on the premises
children.label: f Ineligibility of individuals convicted of manufacturing or producing methamphetamine on the premises

children.text: permanently prohibit occupancy in any public housing dwelling unit by, and assistance under for, any person who has been convicted of manufacturing or otherwise producing methamphetamine on the premises in violation of any Federal or State law; andsection 1437f of this title

immediately and permanently terminate the tenancy in any public housing unit of, and the assistance under for, any person who is convicted of manufacturing or otherwise producing methamphetamine on the premises in violation of any Federal or State law.section 1437f of this title
